about: Committed eval baseline for the regression gate (scripts/eval_regression.py). It pins the SCORING-side faithfulness metrics at the current state of the deterministic grounding scorer (floor/grounding.py) over the committed corpora and model-output caches, so a prompt or scoring change that silently degrades faithfulness FAILS CI. The gate recomputes each metric keyless and offline (no model call, no key) and compares it against this file. Every recorded number was measured from the current scoring state with py scripts/eval_regression.py --record. The gate is direction-aware: a metric where HIGHER is better (corpus precision and recall, per-model materiality accuracy and faithfulness rate) must not fall below its floor or drop more than the tolerance below its baseline; a metric where LOWER is better (the per-model ungrounded / count / date / actor error rates over the clean cached filings) must not rise above its ceiling or climb more than the tolerance above its baseline. TOLERANCE is the absolute slack allowed around each baseline before the gate fails; FLOOR (for higher-is-better) and CEILING (for lower-is-better) are the explicit hard bounds a metric may never cross regardless of tolerance. The floors are set below the recorded baseline by a deliberate margin so an intended, benign re-tuning has room, while a real degradation (the scorer losing recall, or newly false-flagging clean filings) trips the gate. To refresh after an intended scoring change, re-run with --record and review the diff before committing.

corpus.source: tests/fixtures/grounding_corpus.json scored by floor/grounding.score_filing (the same scoring scripts/grounding_report.py --eval reports). 'positive' = the scorer flagged the filing (grounding score below 1.0); ground truth = the human label.

leaderboard.source: tests/fixtures/leaderboard_cache.json scored by scripts/model_leaderboard.score over tests/fixtures/materiality_corpus.json. Per-model error rates over the cached filings; LOWER is better (the rate is how often the frozen oracle flagged a load-bearing span the cached filing could not ground).

open_vs_closed.source: tests/fixtures/open_vs_closed_cache.json scored by scripts/open_vs_closed.score. Per-model faithfulness rate (fraction of cached filings the frozen grounding oracle finds fully grounded) and materiality accuracy (vs the human label); HIGHER is better for both.
